name: Monitor crypto prices with HTTP / REST API and Twilio

parameters.message: =✅ Monitor crypto prices with HTTP / REST API and Twilio — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Every Few Minutes

Starts the workflow on a timer — every 15 min. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Probe Endpoint A

### Probe Endpoint A

GET https://service-a.example.com/healthz — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Next: Combine Probe Results

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Probe Endpoint B

GET https://service-b.example.com/healthz — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Next: Combine Probe Results

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Combine Probe Results

Rejoins 2 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: All Healthy?

### All Healthy?

Two-way gate on `={{ ($json.statusCode || 200) < 400 }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Record Uptime Metric · ②→ Already Alerted? (State Check)

### Record Uptime Metric

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Next: Send Summary to Twilio

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Already Alerted? (State Check)

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Page On-Call via Twilio

### Page On-Call via Twilio

twilio — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Twilio SID + token

### Send Summary to Twilio

twilio — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Twilio SID + token

# Monitor crypto prices with HTTP / REST API and Twilio

**Pattern:** Trading Alert · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Monitor crypto prices with HTTP / REST API and Twilio. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual risk monitoring is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Every Few Minutes → Probe Endpoint A → Combine Probe Results → All Healthy? → Record Uptime Metric → Send Summary to Twilio

## Use it for
- Teams that want to trading alert without manual effort
- Anyone automating risk monitoring in finance
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Scales without extra headcount
- Improves response time
- Keeps your systems in sync

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2 / service account; Twilio SID + token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your risk monitoring process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
